metadata.title: MLCB 2024 Knowledge Graph
metadata.course: Machine Learning for Computational Biology, MIT Fall 2024

nodes.label: Representation Learning
nodes.description: Learning to map raw input data into a compact, informative vector (embedding) that captures the underlying structure. Central to all deep learning approaches in the course.

nodes.label: Latent Space
nodes.description: The low-dimensional continuous space of learned representations. Points in latent space correspond to semantic positions; similar entities cluster together.

nodes.label: Self-Supervised Learning
nodes.description: A training paradigm where supervision signal is derived from the data itself (e.g., masked token prediction) without human-labeled annotations. Key for genomic and protein language models.

nodes.label: Multimodal Foundation Models
nodes.description: Large pre-trained models that integrate multiple data modalities (images, sequences, graphs) into a shared representation space. Emerging approach in computational biology.

nodes.label: Data-Driven Paradigm
nodes.description: Scientific methodology that derives hypotheses and conclusions from patterns in large datasets, contrasted with hypothesis-driven approaches that start with a biological question.

nodes.label: Hypothesis-Driven Paradigm
nodes.description: Scientific methodology that formulates a specific biological hypothesis and designs experiments to test it, contrasted with data-driven pattern discovery.

nodes.label: Graph Neural Network
nodes.description: A neural network architecture that operates on graph-structured data by iteratively aggregating neighbor node features via message passing. Used for molecular property prediction, regulatory network analysis, and protein interaction modeling.

nodes.label: Large Language Model
nodes.description: A neural network trained on massive text corpora to model language. In computational biology, LLM architectures are adapted for biological sequences (proteins, DNA, RNA).

nodes.label: Gibbs Sampling
nodes.description: A Markov chain Monte Carlo algorithm that samples from joint distributions by iteratively sampling each variable conditioned on all others. Used in motif finding and graphical model inference.

nodes.label: Expectation-Maximization
nodes.description: An iterative optimization algorithm for maximum likelihood estimation with latent variables. Alternates between computing expected latent assignments (E-step) and maximizing parameters (M-step). Used in GMMs, HMMs, and motif finding.

nodes.label: Gene Expression Matrix
nodes.description: A cells-by-genes (or samples-by-genes) matrix of RNA quantification values. The fundamental input data type for transcriptomics clustering and single-cell analysis.

nodes.label: RNA-seq
nodes.description: Sequencing-based measurement of the total transcriptome of a cell population. Produces a gene-by-sample count matrix. Predecessor to single-cell methods.

nodes.label: K-Means Clustering
nodes.description: An iterative partitioning algorithm that assigns N data points to K clusters by alternating between centroid assignment and centroid update steps. Minimizes within-cluster sum of squared distances.

nodes.label: Gaussian Mixture Model
nodes.description: A probabilistic model representing a distribution as a weighted sum of Gaussian components. Fit by EM; provides soft cluster assignments and likelihood scores, unlike k-means.

nodes.label: Principal Component Analysis
nodes.description: Linear dimensionality reduction that finds orthogonal axes of maximum variance. Standard preprocessing step before clustering and visualization in transcriptomics.

nodes.label: UMAP
nodes.description: Nonlinear dimensionality reduction method that preserves local and global structure of high-dimensional data. Widely used for 2D visualization of single-cell datasets.

nodes.label: t-SNE
nodes.description: Nonlinear dimensionality reduction optimizing pairwise similarity in 2D. Effective for local cluster visualization but distorts global distances. Often replaced by UMAP.

nodes.label: scRNA-seq
nodes.description: Sequencing the transcriptome of individual cells rather than bulk tissue. Enables cell-type identification, trajectory inference, and heterogeneity analysis.

nodes.label: Cell Type Annotation
nodes.description: Assigning biological cell type labels to clusters identified from single-cell data. Uses marker gene expression patterns as ground truth.

nodes.label: Trajectory Inference
nodes.description: Computational inference of differentiation paths from single-cell snapshots. Orders cells along a developmental trajectory without temporal data.

nodes.label: Scanpy
nodes.description: Python toolkit for single-cell RNA-seq analysis. Implements preprocessing, clustering, UMAP, trajectory analysis, and more via AnnData objects.

nodes.label: Sequence Alignment
nodes.description: Computing the optimal correspondence between two biological sequences (DNA, RNA, or protein) to maximize similarity. Includes global (Needleman-Wunsch) and local (Smith-Waterman) variants.

nodes.label: Dynamic Programming
nodes.description: Algorithmic paradigm that solves complex problems by breaking them into overlapping subproblems and caching results. Underlies sequence alignment, HMM decoding, and RNA structure prediction.

nodes.label: BLAST
nodes.description: Heuristic sequence database search tool. Uses word-based seeding to find local alignments much faster than Smith-Waterman at modest sensitivity cost. The standard tool for homology search.

nodes.label: Scoring Matrix
nodes.description: A matrix of log-odds scores for amino acid or nucleotide substitution pairs, derived from observed frequencies in evolutionary alignments. PAM and BLOSUM are the standard protein scoring matrices.

nodes.label: Motif Finding
nodes.description: Discovering short conserved sequence patterns (motifs) in biological sequences. EM and Gibbs sampling underpin probabilistic motif models like MEME.

nodes.label: MEME
nodes.description: Software suite for motif discovery and enrichment analysis in DNA and protein sequences. Uses EM for unsupervised motif learning.

nodes.label: Hidden Markov Model
nodes.description: A probabilistic graphical model with hidden states and observed emissions. States represent biological annotations (e.g., chromatin states, exon/intron), observations are genomic signals.

nodes.label: Viterbi Algorithm
nodes.description: Dynamic programming algorithm for finding the most probable hidden state sequence in an HMM given a sequence of observations.

nodes.label: Baum-Welch Algorithm
nodes.description: EM algorithm for estimating HMM parameters (transition and emission probabilities) from unlabeled observation sequences.

nodes.label: Chromatin
nodes.description: The complex of DNA and histone proteins that packages the genome in eukaryotic nuclei. Chromatin accessibility and modification state regulate gene expression.

nodes.label: ChIP-seq
nodes.description: Sequencing-based assay that maps protein-DNA binding sites genome-wide by immunoprecipitating chromatin with a target-specific antibody.

nodes.label: ATAC-seq
nodes.description: Sequencing-based assay that measures open chromatin (accessible regions) genome-wide using a hyperactive Tn5 transposase.

nodes.label: Histone Modification
nodes.description: Covalent post-translational modifications on histone tails (methylation, acetylation, etc.) that regulate chromatin state and gene expression. Key epigenomic marks measured by ChIP-seq.

nodes.label: ChromHMM
nodes.description: Software for learning and analyzing chromatin state annotations using multi-variate HMMs trained on histone modification ChIP-seq tracks.

nodes.label: DNA Methylation
nodes.description: Addition of a methyl group to cytosine (primarily at CpG sites) in DNA. Stable epigenetic mark associated with gene silencing and genome stability.

nodes.label: Transcription Factor
nodes.description: Protein that binds specific DNA sequences (motifs) to activate or repress gene transcription. Central to gene regulatory networks.

nodes.label: Enhancer
nodes.description: A regulatory DNA element that activates target gene expression from long distances by looping to the promoter. Identified by H3K27ac and accessibility marks.

nodes.label: Promoter
nodes.description: DNA region immediately upstream of a gene where transcription initiation machinery assembles. Marked by H3K4me3 and CpG islands.

nodes.label: Gene Regulatory Network
nodes.description: A network model where nodes are genes and transcription factors and edges represent regulatory interactions (activation or repression). Encodes the logic of cellular identity.

nodes.label: Network Inference
nodes.description: Computational methods to reconstruct gene regulatory networks from expression data. Includes correlation-based, mutual information, and regression approaches.

nodes.label: Network Motif
nodes.description: Recurring subgraph pattern in biological networks that performs specific functional roles (e.g., feed-forward loop, autoregulation). Identified by comparing network to random graphs.

nodes.label: Protein Structure
nodes.description: The three-dimensional arrangement of atoms in a protein, organized into primary (sequence), secondary (helix/sheet), tertiary (fold), and quaternary (complex) levels.

nodes.label: Alpha Helix
nodes.description: A right-handed helical secondary structure element stabilized by backbone hydrogen bonds between residues i and i+4. One of two primary protein secondary structures.

nodes.label: Beta Sheet
nodes.description: Protein secondary structure formed by hydrogen-bonded extended polypeptide strands. Can be parallel or antiparallel.

nodes.label: Protein Contact Map
nodes.description: Binary or continuous matrix encoding which residue pairs are spatially close (< 8Å) in the folded protein. Key intermediate representation for structure prediction.

nodes.label: Multiple Sequence Alignment
nodes.description: Simultaneous alignment of three or more homologous sequences. Encodes evolutionary conservation and covariation patterns. Primary input to AlphaFold2.

nodes.label: Coevolution Analysis
nodes.description: Statistical analysis of correlated mutations across homologous sequences to infer residue-residue contacts. Underpins pre-deep-learning contact prediction.

nodes.label: AlphaFold2
nodes.description: DeepMind's protein structure prediction model (2021) that achieves experimental-level accuracy by combining MSA processing through Evoformer with structure module refinement.

nodes.label: Evoformer
nodes.description: The core transformer module of AlphaFold2 that jointly processes multiple sequence alignments and pairwise residue representations using axial attention.

nodes.label: Attention Mechanism
nodes.description: Neural mechanism that computes pairwise relevance scores between sequence positions using query-key-value projections. Core to Transformers, AlphaFold2, and protein/DNA language models.

nodes.label: lDDT Score
nodes.description: Per-residue accuracy metric for predicted protein structures. Measures fraction of predicted inter-residue distances within a tolerance of the true distances. Used by AlphaFold2 as confidence score.

nodes.label: ESM-2
nodes.description: Meta AI's protein language model trained on 250M protein sequences with masked language modeling. Learns structural information from sequence alone; zero-shot fitness prediction.

nodes.label: Masked Language Modeling
nodes.description: Pre-training objective where random tokens are masked and the model predicts the original tokens. Enables self-supervised learning from unannotated sequences.

nodes.label: Zero-Shot Fitness Prediction
nodes.description: Predicting the effect of protein mutations on fitness using log-likelihood scores from a pretrained language model, without any task-specific fine-tuning.

nodes.label: Protein Sequence
nodes.description: Linear sequence of amino acids encoding a protein, written in one-letter codes. Input to protein language models and structure prediction tools.

nodes.label: DNABERT
nodes.description: BERT-based model pre-trained on genomic DNA sequences with k-mer tokenization. Learns nucleotide context representations for promoter prediction, splice site detection, and TF binding.

nodes.label: Nucleotide Transformer
nodes.description: Large genomic language model trained on multi-species genomes. InstaDeep/EMBL model demonstrating transferability across genomic prediction tasks.

nodes.label: HyenaDNA
nodes.description: Long-range genomic sequence model using Hyena operators (implicit convolutions) instead of attention, enabling context windows of 1 million+ nucleotides.

nodes.label: Caduceus
nodes.description: Bidirectional DNA language model using Mamba state-space model architecture with reverse-complement equivariance. Achieves long-context genomic modeling.

nodes.label: DNA Sequence
nodes.description: Linear sequence of nucleotides (A, T, C, G) encoding the genome. Input to sequence alignment, motif finding, epigenomics pipelines, and DNA language models.

nodes.label: Drug Discovery
nodes.description: The process of identifying, validating, and optimizing small molecules or biologics to treat disease. Computational methods accelerate target identification, virtual screening, and ADMET prediction.

nodes.label: ADMET
nodes.description: Absorption, Distribution, Metabolism, Excretion, and Toxicity — the key pharmacokinetic properties a drug candidate must satisfy for clinical viability.

nodes.label: Virtual Screening
nodes.description: Computational evaluation of large compound libraries to identify candidates that bind a target protein. Includes docking-based and ML-based approaches.

nodes.label: SMILES
aliases: Simplified Molecular Input Line Entry System
nodes.description: Line notation for representing molecular structure using ASCII characters. The most common molecular string representation. Limitations include non-unique strings and occasional invalid chemistries.

nodes.label: SELFIES
nodes.description: Molecular string representation where every valid string corresponds to a valid molecule. Designed for generative models to avoid chemically invalid outputs.

nodes.label: Molecular Graph
nodes.description: Graph-based representation of a molecule where atoms are nodes and bonds are edges. Enables GNN-based molecular property prediction.

nodes.label: Message Passing Neural Network
nodes.description: GNN variant that updates node representations by iteratively aggregating and transforming neighbor messages. Foundational architecture for molecular property prediction.

nodes.label: SchNet
nodes.description: Deep tensor neural network for learning continuous-filter convolutions on molecular geometries in 3D space. Predicts quantum chemical properties.

nodes.label: Variational Autoencoder
nodes.description: Deep generative model that encodes data to a probabilistic latent space and decodes samples from it. Used for molecule generation and continuous molecular optimization.

nodes.label: Diffusion Model
nodes.description: Generative model that learns to reverse a noise-adding diffusion process. Achieves state-of-the-art molecule generation in 3D space (e.g., GEOM-Drugs benchmarks).

nodes.label: Normalizing Flow
nodes.description: Generative model using invertible transformations to map between data and latent distributions, enabling exact likelihood computation.

nodes.label: Reinforcement Learning for Drug Design
nodes.description: Using RL reward signals (docking scores, ADMET predictions) to guide generative models toward desired molecular properties.

nodes.label: Backpropagation
nodes.description: Algorithm for computing gradients of a loss function with respect to neural network weights via the chain rule of calculus. Enables efficient training of deep networks.

nodes.label: Batch Normalization
nodes.description: Normalization technique that standardizes layer inputs per mini-batch. Accelerates training, reduces internal covariate shift, and acts as a regularizer.

nodes.label: Dropout
nodes.description: Regularization technique that randomly zeroes activations during training to prevent co-adaptation and reduce overfitting.

nodes.label: Adam Optimizer
nodes.description: Adaptive learning rate optimizer combining momentum and RMSProp. Default optimizer for most deep learning in biology; AdamW adds weight decay.

nodes.label: Transfer Learning
nodes.description: Reusing weights learned on a large source task (e.g., protein language model) and adapting them to a target task with fewer examples.

nodes.label: Genome-Wide Association Study
nodes.description: Statistical study that tests millions of genetic variants (SNPs) across the genome for association with a phenotype or disease in a case-control or population cohort.

nodes.label: Single Nucleotide Polymorphism
nodes.description: A single base-pair change in DNA sequence that differs between individuals. The primary unit of genetic variation analyzed in GWAS.

nodes.label: Polygenic Risk Score
nodes.description: Weighted sum of an individual's risk alleles across the genome, with weights derived from GWAS effect sizes. Quantifies genetic predisposition to complex traits.

nodes.label: Linkage Disequilibrium
nodes.description: Non-random association between alleles at different genomic positions due to limited recombination. Defines the correlation structure that complicates causal variant identification.

nodes.label: Expression Quantitative Trait Locus
nodes.description: Genetic variant associated with gene expression levels in a tissue. Links GWAS disease variants to regulatory mechanisms and gene targets.

nodes.label: Heritability
nodes.description: Proportion of phenotypic variance attributable to genetic variation. Narrow-sense heritability estimated from GWAS data using LD Score Regression.

nodes.label: LD Score Regression
nodes.description: Method to estimate SNP heritability and genetic correlation from GWAS summary statistics using LD scores as weights. Avoids confounding from population stratification.

nodes.label: Colocalization Analysis
nodes.description: Statistical method to test whether a GWAS signal and an eQTL signal in the same locus share a causal variant, distinguishing true colocalization from LD-driven coincidence.

nodes.label: Fine-Mapping
nodes.description: Statistical methods to identify the most likely causal variants within a GWAS locus by computing posterior inclusion probabilities using LD patterns.

nodes.label: Mendelian Randomization
nodes.description: Method that uses genetic variants as instrumental variables to infer causal effects of exposures on outcomes, analogous to a natural randomized controlled trial.

nodes.label: Disease Circuitry
nodes.description: The regulatory network — enhancers, TFs, target genes — that is perturbed by disease-associated variants. Connecting GWAS loci to epigenomics reveals causal mechanisms.

nodes.label: Deep Learning
nodes.description: Machine learning using multi-layer neural networks that learn hierarchical representations. Umbrella concept covering CNNs, RNNs, Transformers, GNNs, and generative models.

nodes.label: Transformer
nodes.description: Neural network architecture based on self-attention that processes sequences in parallel. Underpins GPT, BERT, ESM, AlphaFold2, DNABERT, and most modern biological sequence models.

nodes.label: Gene
nodes.description: A hereditary unit of DNA that encodes a functional product (typically a protein or non-coding RNA). Genes are regulated by promoters, enhancers, and TFs.

nodes.label: Protein
nodes.description: A biological macromolecule made of amino acids that performs structural, enzymatic, signaling, and regulatory functions. Three-dimensional structure determines function.

nodes.label: DNA
nodes.description: Double-stranded polymer of nucleotides encoding the genome. Substrate for transcription, replication, and epigenomic modification.

nodes.label: RNA
nodes.description: Intermediary between DNA and protein (mRNA) or functional molecule in its own right (lncRNA, miRNA, rRNA). Measured by RNA-seq and scRNA-seq.

nodes.label: Small Molecule
nodes.description: Low-molecular-weight organic compound (typically <500 Da) that can modulate biological activity. The primary drug modality in pharmaceutical development.

nodes.label: Drug Target
nodes.description: A biological molecule (usually protein) whose activity is modulated by a drug to achieve a therapeutic effect. Target identification is the first step in drug discovery.

nodes.label: Molecular Docking
nodes.description: Computational prediction of how a small molecule binds to a protein target in 3D space. Used in virtual screening to rank compound libraries.

nodes.label: Evidence Lower Bound
nodes.description: Objective function for VAEs: ELBO = E[log p(x|z)] - KL(q(z|x)||p(z)). Maximizing ELBO is equivalent to maximizing a lower bound on log p(x).

nodes.label: Cross-Entropy Loss
nodes.description: Standard classification loss: -Σ y_i log(p_i). Used in masked language modeling, sequence classification, and most neural network training in the course.

nodes.label: Manolis Kellis
nodes.description: Professor at MIT, co-instructor of MLCB 2024. Research areas: computational biology, genomics, epigenomics, disease circuitry, protein structure.

nodes.label: Eric Alm
nodes.description: Co-instructor of MLCB 2024. Research areas: microbiome, generative AI, computational biology.

nodes.label: Computational Biology
nodes.description: Interdisciplinary field applying computational methods to biological questions. MLCB course covers the major subfields: genomics, proteomics, epigenomics, drug discovery, genetics.

nodes.label: Module 1: Genomics and Regulatory Networks
nodes.description: MLCB Module 1 covering gene expression, single-cell analysis, alignment, epigenomics, and regulatory networks (Lectures 2-7).

nodes.label: Module 2: Protein Structure and Language Models
nodes.description: MLCB Module 2 covering protein structure, folding algorithms, Transformers for structure, and protein/DNA language models (Lectures 8-12).

nodes.label: Module 3: Chemistry and Drug Discovery
nodes.description: MLCB Module 3 covering drug development, molecular GNNs, generative molecule design (Lectures 13-15).

nodes.label: Module 4: Genetics and Disease
nodes.description: MLCB Module 4 covering training neural networks, genetics, GWAS, PRS, eQTLs, and disease mechanism (Lectures 16-18).

nodes.label: InChI
nodes.description: Standardized unique chemical identifier providing a one-to-one correspondence between a molecule and a string. Used for database disambiguation.

nodes.label: SMARTS
nodes.description: Pattern language extending SMILES for substructure searching. Enables identification of chemical functional groups within molecular databases.

nodes.label: Rosetta
nodes.description: Software suite for macromolecular structure prediction and design using energy-based scoring functions. Pre-deep-learning gold standard for protein structure prediction.

nodes.label: AlphaFold (v1)
nodes.description: DeepMind's first protein structure prediction model (CASP13, 2018) using gradient-based geometry optimization with learned distance distributions.

nodes.label: CASP
aliases: Critical Assessment of Protein Structure Prediction
nodes.description: Biennial blind prediction competition for protein structure prediction. AlphaFold2 achieved human expert-level accuracy at CASP14 (2020), transforming the field.

nodes.label: DimeNet
nodes.description: Graph neural network for molecules that incorporates 3D geometric information (bond angles) via directional message passing, improving molecular property prediction.

nodes.label: ENCODE
nodes.description: Large-scale genomics project generating ChIP-seq, ATAC-seq, and RNA-seq data across human cell types. Primary data source for regulatory element annotation.

nodes.label: SCENIC
nodes.description: Tool for single-cell regulatory network inference. Combines GRN inference with regulon activity scoring to identify cell-type-specific TF programs.

nodes.label: Stochastic Gradient Descent
nodes.description: Optimization algorithm that updates model parameters using gradients estimated from mini-batches of data. Fundamental training algorithm for neural networks.

nodes.label: Manhattan Plot
nodes.description: Visualization of GWAS results plotting -log10(p-value) per SNP across chromosomes. Peaks indicate associated genomic loci.

nodes.label: CpG Island
nodes.description: Genomic region with high CpG dinucleotide density, typically at gene promoters. Often unmethylated at active promoters; methylation silences gene expression.

nodes.label: Protein Design
nodes.description: Designing novel proteins with desired structures or functions. Language models and diffusion models (RFDiffusion) enable sequence-structure co-design.

nodes.label: Benchmarking
nodes.description: Standardized datasets and metrics for comparing model performance. Critical for measuring progress in protein structure (CASP), molecular generation (GuacaMol), and genomics (ENCODE challenges).
